marketState: neutral - conflicting signals, bullish near-term weather offset by bearish storage/technical trend
summary: Price ($3.206) sits modestly above modeled fair value ($2.929, band $2.429-$3.429) but that gap closes to 'FAIR' by day 14-21 as the fair-value estimate itself drifts up to ~$3.07, so there is no large mispricing to trade. Seasonality is a mild headwind (price -2.5pp vs median, only 31-50% odds of being higher over 1-4 weeks, typical 4-week move -3.1%) but the model flags reliability as Low (50pp spread), so this carries limited weight. Weather is the standout signal: 8-16 day cooling demand is +46% vs the 5-year norm and above the historical max range, with the trend already up +7.3 in 24h and +29% vs normal - a genuine near-term bullish catalyst. Working against that is a bearish storage print (+87 Bcf build, +13 Bcf surprise-bearish, and +175 Bcf above the 5-year average, i.e. ample cushion), a moderately net-short managed-money position, and daily/weekly technicals in a bearish trend (50<200 EMA cross) even though short-term (5m/15m) is oversold (RSI ~27-32, below lower Bollinger). The futures curve shows a normal seasonal shape (slight backwardation into Sep/Oct, sharp winter contango into Jan, spring shoulder lows) rather than a distorted one, which is itself not a strong directional signal.

longTerm.rationale: Seasonality points modestly lower but is explicitly Low reliability, storage is comfortably above the 5-year average (bearish) and COT shows managed money already net short, while weather demand 8-16D is unusually strong (bullish) - these roughly offset and there is no edge from the fair-value band (price is only 'SLIGHTLY OVER' now and 'FAIR' by day 14-21).
longTerm.trigger: Wait for either (a) a pullback toward the lower fair-value band (~$2.55-2.70) to buy value, or (b) confirmation that the elevated 8-16D heat is drawing storage below the 5y-average surplus (a bullish catalyst), or (c) a daily/weekly close that breaks the current EMA 50<200 downtrend structure to justify a short with better trend confirmation.

shortTerm.rationale: 5m/15m are oversold (RSI 27-32, price below lower Bollinger) into an extreme, still-rising 8-16D cooling-demand forecast (+46% vs 5y, above historical max), and price is only slightly rich to fair value with room up to the $3.43 band edge before day-14/21 fair value catches up near $3.04-3.07.
shortTerm.invalidation: A daily close below $3.02 (below the current fair-value band low and confirming the 1d/1w bearish EMA cross continuing) invalidates the bounce thesis.

keyDrivers: 8-16 day cooling demand +46% vs 5y and above historical max - strongest single signal, near-term bullish
Price only slightly over modeled fair value; band converges to 'FAIR' by day 14-21
Storage surplus (+175 Bcf vs 5y) and bearish build surprise (+13 Bcf) cap upside medium-term
Daily/weekly technicals bearish (50<200 EMA) but short-frame (5m/15m) oversold
Seasonality mildly bearish but flagged Low reliability (50pp spread)
risks: Weather forecasts beyond ~10 days are unreliable and could revert quickly, removing the main bullish prop
Storage surplus plus already-bearish daily/weekly trend could accelerate a breakdown if heat fails to materialize
